channel.title: WinAPI on Remko's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in WinAPI on Remko's Blog

item.title: Convert Drive Bitmask to Drive Letter
item.description:
I was writing a test program that will perform some actions when a USB Memory Stick is inserted.

When this happens Windows send a Broadcast a WM_DEVICECHANGE message.

The wParam member of this Message contains a (pointer to) a DEV_BROADCAST_HDR structure.

if the dbch_devicetype member of this structure is of type DBT_DEVTYP_VOLUME then we can cast the structure to DEV_BROADCAST_VOLUME.

item.title: Converting a volume name to a device name
item.description:
Windows has a couple of different formats for volume names but it is unclear how to convert a Volumename (example: \?\Volume{GUID}) to a DeviceName (example: \Device\HarddiskVolume1).

I found at that you can use the QueryDosDevice function but you need to remove the preceeding \?\ and the trailing \ of the VolumeName:
